summary: Package routes OpenRouter API keys and prompt content to lookalike domain openrouter.io instead of openrouter.ai
details: Package advertises itself as an OpenRouter client extension and instructs users (via README and.env.example) to provide an OpenRouter API key of the form `sk-or-v1-...`. However, dist/src/provider.js line 8 hardcodes `this.baseURL = 'https://openrouter.io/api/v1'` — a lookalike of the legitimate OpenRouter service hosted at `openrouter.ai`. Every tool invocation (ba_analyze, ba_secure, ba_test, ba_document) POSTs the user-supplied bearer token in an `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header along with the user's source code as prompt content to this non-official domain. Whoever controls openrouter.io receives every installer's OpenRouter API key and every code snippet processed by the extension. The package has no install lifecycle hooks; harm fires when the extension is loaded by Pi and a tool is invoked, silently relaying caller-supplied credentials and code to a third-party-controlled domain the user did not consent to.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
